docs.title: YesDR Standard
docs.text:
YesDR (pronounced “Yes Dee Are”) Your Extensible Software Defined Radio

YesDR is a modular, extensible cellular wireless standard designed for research, education, and innovation across the PHY, RAN, and Core Network, with native support for AI-driven radio intelligence.

docs.title: Key Objectives
docs.text:
• End-to-end cellular experimentation using SDRs
  • AI-enabled spectrum sensing and protocol optimization
  • Open academic and industry collaboration
  • Lightweight alternative to full 3GPP stacks

docs.title: What You’ll Find Here
docs.text:
• Official YesDR Technical Specifications (TS)
  • Architecture and protocol definitions
  • Consortium and roadmap information
  • Reference implementations and products

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: YESDR covers: - Physical layer waveforms and coding - RAN protocols and scheduling - Core network control and user plane - AI-driven spectrum and protocol optimization

docs.title: About YesDR
docs.text:
YESDR is a standards-oriented framework for designing, implementing, and validating cellular wireless protocols using Software Defined Radios.

It is designed to complement—not replace—3GPP systems, enabling rapid experimentation and education.

docs.title: Pronunciation and Naming
docs.text:
• Written form: YESDR
  • Spoken form: Yes Dee Are
  • Expanded form: Your Extensible Software Defined Radio

The grouping YES | DR is intentional to aid pronunciation and recall.

• Release-1 specifications
  • Reference Python implementations
  • Academic pilots

• C++ core
  • ORAN and MEC integration
  • Multi-lab deployments
• National-scale 6G testbed

docs.title: Membership Application Policy
docs.text: Document Status: Informative Effective From: [To be announced] Approved By: YesDR Consortium Steering Committee

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text: This Membership Application Policy defines the procedures, requirements, and evaluation process for organizations and individuals seeking membership in the YesDR Consortium. The policy ensures a transparent, fair, and structured approach to consortium participation.

docs.title: 2. Scope
docs.text: This policy applies to all applicants requesting membership in the YesDR Consortium, including academic institutions, startups, industry participants, and individual contributors.

docs.title: 3.1 General Eligibility
docs.text: Applicants must: - Support the objectives and mission of the YesDR Consortium - Agree to comply with consortium policies, including governance, contribution, and intellectual property rules - Demonstrate a legitimate interest in cellular wireless systems, SDR, 5G/6G, or related technologies

docs.title: Academic Members
docs.text:
• Accredited educational or research institutions
  • Engagement in teaching or research related to wireless communication, networking, or AI-driven systems

docs.title: Startup / SME Members
docs.text:
• Legally registered organizations
  • Active or planned work in wireless, SDR, 5G/6G, or AI-based communication systems

docs.title: Industry Members
docs.text:
• Established companies in telecom, networking, hardware, software, or related domains
  • Interest in evaluation, deployment, or contribution to YesDR technologies

docs.title: Individual Members
docs.text:
• Independent researchers, faculty, students, or professionals
  • Participation in a non-commercial or limited individual capacity

docs.title: Strategic / Founding Members
docs.text: • Organizations providing significant technical, infrastructure, or financial contributions to the YesDR ecosystem

docs.title: 4.1 Submission
docs.text:
Applicants shall submit a membership application containing: - Applicant name and legal status - Membership category requested - Brief description of technical background and interest in YesDR - Intended level of participation (research, development, review, deployment) - Primary contact details

Applications shall be submitted through the official YesDR Consortium channel (e.g., website form or designated email address).

docs.title: 4.2 Review
docs.text:
• Applications are reviewed by the YesDR Consortium Steering Committee or a delegated review panel.
  • The review process evaluates alignment with consortium objectives and eligibility criteria.
  • Additional information may be requested from the applicant if required.

docs.title: 4.3 Decision
docs.text:
• Applicants will be notified of acceptance, conditional acceptance, or rejection.
  • The consortium reserves the right to accept or reject applications at its discretion.
  • Decisions are final and not subject to appeal.

docs.title: 5. Membership Activation
docs.text:
Membership becomes active upon: - Formal acceptance by the consortium - Completion of required documentation - Payment of applicable membership fees (if any)

Access to member-specific benefits is granted only after activation.

docs.title: 6.1 Applicant Responsibilities
docs.text:
• Provide accurate and complete information
  • Comply with consortium policies upon acceptance
  • Maintain updated contact information

docs.title: 6.2 Consortium Rights
docs.text:
• Request clarifications or supporting documents
  • Modify application requirements as needed
  • Limit the number of members in specific categories if required for governance

docs.title: 7. Confidentiality
docs.text: Information submitted during the application process is treated as confidential and used solely for membership evaluation and administration purposes.

docs.title: 8. Changes to Membership Category
docs.text: Members may request a change in membership category by submitting a revised application. Such requests are subject to review and approval.

docs.title: 9. Policy Review and Updates
docs.text: This policy may be updated periodically to reflect changes in consortium structure or operational requirements. Updated versions will be published on the YesDR Consortium website.

docs.title: 10. Disclaimer
docs.text: Submission of a membership application does not guarantee acceptance. Membership does not confer ownership or exclusive rights over the YesDR standard or its specifications.

docs.title: Membership Fee Policy
docs.text: Document Status: Informative Effective From: [To be announced] Approved By: YesDR Consortium Steering Committee

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text: This Membership Fee Policy defines the fee structure, payment terms, and financial principles governing participation in the YesDR Consortium. The policy aims to ensure sustainable consortium operations while preserving accessibility for academic institutions, researchers, and innovation-driven organizations.

docs.title: 2. Scope
docs.text: This policy applies to all organizations and individuals seeking membership in the YesDR Consortium, including academic institutions, startups, industry partners, and individual contributors.

docs.title: 3. Membership Categories
docs.text: The YesDR Consortium recognizes the following membership categories:

docs.title: 3.1 Academic Member
docs.text:
• Universities
  • Engineering colleges
  • Public research institutions
  • Government-funded laboratories

docs.title: 3.2 Startup / SME Member
docs.text:
• Early-stage startups
  • Small and medium enterprises
  • Organizations working on SDR, 5G/6G, AI, and wireless systems

docs.title: 3.3 Industry Member
docs.text:
• Large enterprises
  • Telecom vendors
  • System integrators
  • Platform and semiconductor providers

docs.title: 3.4 Individual Member
docs.text:
• Independent researchers
  • Faculty members
  • Graduate and postgraduate students
  • Professionals participating in an individual capacity

docs.title: 3.5 Strategic / Founding Member
docs.text: • Organizations providing significant technical, financial, or infrastructure contributions to the YesDR ecosystem

docs.title: 4. Membership Fee Structure
docs.text:
Membership Category Annual Fee Remarks Academic Member Nil / Nominal Encouraged for education and research Individual Member Nominal Non-commercial participation Startup / SME Member Moderate Scaled for early-stage organizations Industry Member Standard Commercial participation Strategic / Founding Member By agreement Customized contribution

Note: Fee amounts are published separately and may vary based on region, consortium phase, or strategic considerations.

docs.title: 5. Fee Principles
docs.text:
The following principles govern membership fees:

  • Membership fees are charged on an annual basis unless otherwise specified.
  • Fees are non-refundable once membership is activated.
  • Fee waivers or reductions may be granted to academic or non-profit entities.
  • Membership fees are reviewed periodically by the Steering Committee.
  • Public access to released YesDR specifications does not require membership.

docs.title: 6. Use of Membership Fees
docs.text:
Membership fees are used exclusively to support YesDR Consortium activities, including but not limited to:

  • Development and maintenance of YesDR specifications
  • Documentation, website, and hosting infrastructure
  • Reference implementations and tooling support
  • Workshops, tutorials, and outreach programs
  • Administrative and governance operations

docs.title: 7. Membership Benefits
docs.text:
Depending on the membership category, benefits may include:

  • Participation in technical working groups
  • Early access to draft specifications
  • Voting rights in consortium decisions (where applicable)
  • Recognition on the YesDR Consortium website
  • Access to member-only discussions and reviews

Specific benefits are defined separately for each membership category.

docs.title: 8. Fee Review and Revision
docs.text:
• Membership fees may be revised periodically.
  • Any revisions will be communicated in advance.
  • Existing members may be provided a transition period where applicable.

docs.title: 9. Compliance and Termination
docs.text:
• Membership may be suspended or terminated in case of non-payment.
  • Termination does not affect previously published public contributions.
  • Membership may be reinstated upon settlement of outstanding dues.

docs.title: 10. Transparency
docs.text: The YesDR Consortium is committed to transparent financial governance. High-level summaries of membership fee utilization may be shared with members periodically.

docs.title: 11. Disclaimer
docs.text: Payment of membership fees does not imply ownership or exclusive control of the YesDR standard. All specifications remain governed by the consortium under applicable licensing, contribution, and governance policies.

docs.title: YesDR Wireshark Dissectors
docs.text:
YesDR provides reference Wireshark dissectors for its control-plane protocols to support protocol inspection, debugging, validation, and educational use.

The dissectors are aligned with the corresponding YesDR Technical Specifications and are provided as reference implementations.

docs.title: Supported Protocols
docs.text: Protocol YesDR Specification Dissector Language YACP TS 04.001 Lua YSMP TS 04.002 Lua

docs.title: YACP Dissector
docs.text:
Protocol Name: YesDR Access Control Protocol Specification: TS 04.001

The YACP dissector enables decoding of: - Access and registration messages - Information Elements (IEs) - Procedure and cause codes - Transaction identifiers

docs.title: YSMP Dissector
docs.text:
Protocol Name: YesDR Spectrum Management Protocol Specification: TS 04.002

The YSMP dissector enables decoding of: - Spectrum sensing reports - Allocation and control messages - Interference and occupancy fields

docs.title: TS 02.002 — YSMF
docs.text:
Defines session establishment, modification, and release procedures.

Summary: YSMF manages data session lifecycle, IP address allocation, QoS flow control, and association between control-plane and user-plane entities. It plays a role analogous to the 5G SMF, while allowing simplified and extensible session models.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_02_002_YSMF

docs.title: TS 02.003 — YUPF
docs.text:
Specifies user-plane packet handling and forwarding.

Summary: YUPF handles packet routing, encapsulation, tunneling, and QoS enforcement. It supports MEC offloading, experimental data paths, and flexible forwarding rules for research and testbed deployments.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_02_003_YUPF

docs.title: TS 02.004 — YUDM
docs.text:
Defines subscriber, device, and service data storage.

Summary: YUDM maintains user profiles, authentication parameters, session policies, and capability information. It provides a centralized data repository accessible by other core functions.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_02_004_YUDM

docs.title: TS 02.050 — YAUSF
docs.text:
Defines authentication and security procedures.

Summary: YAUSF performs user and device authentication, key derivation, and security context establishment. It supports extensible authentication methods suitable for SDR-based and experimental deployments.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_02_005_YAUSF

docs.title: TS 02.006 — YPCF
docs.text:
Specifies policy generation and enforcement rules.

Summary: YPCF determines QoS policies, access restrictions, traffic prioritization, and charging-related decisions. Policies are applied dynamically to sessions and data flows.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_02_006_YPCF

docs.title: TS 02.007 — YRMF
docs.text:
Defines radio resource and spectrum control.

Summary: YRMF manages transmit power, bandwidth allocation, carrier selection, and radio configuration. It interacts closely with the Cognitive Radio Function for adaptive radio operation.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_02_007_YRMF

docs.title: TS 02.008 — YCRF
docs.text:
Defines AI-driven radio intelligence.

Summary: YCRF enables spectrum sensing, signal classification, channel prediction, traffic forecasting, and dynamic spectrum access. It supports closed-loop optimization using machine learning and AI models.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_02_008_YCRF

docs.title: TS 02.009 — YNRF
docs.text:
Defines service registration and discovery.

Summary: YNRF maintains a registry of available YesDR network functions and services, enabling dynamic discovery, scaling, and service-based interaction between core components.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_02_009_YNRF

docs.title: TS 02.010 — YSM
docs.text:
Defines spectrum monitoring and measurement.

Summary: YSM performs continuous spectrum observation, interference detection, occupancy estimation, and reporting. Measurement outputs may be consumed by YCRF and YRMF.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_02_010_YSM

docs.title: TS 02.020 — YBS
docs.text:
Defines the radio access node behavior.

Summary: YBS provides radio access, scheduling, timing, and control signaling between YUE and the core network. It supports SDR-based PHY/MAC implementations and experimental RAN procedures.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_02_020_YBS

docs.title: TS 02.021 — YUE
docs.text:
Defines UE behavior and protocol stack.

Summary: YUE specifies PHY, MAC, RLC, PDCP, and control-plane behaviors for end devices. It supports SDR-based implementations, experimentation, and AI-assisted PHY processing.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_02_021_YUE

docs.title: TS 04.001 — YACP
docs.text:
Defines access and registration signaling.

Summary: YACP specifies procedures for device registration, authentication initiation, capability exchange, and mobility signaling between YUE, YBS, and YCore. It is conceptually aligned with NGAP while remaining simplified.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_04_001_YACP

docs.title: TS 04.002 — YSMP
docs.text:
Defines spectrum coordination signaling.

Summary: YSMP enables spectrum usage reporting, allocation commands, interference alerts, and coordination between YSM, YRMF, YCRF, and YBS. It supports dynamic and AI-driven spectrum control.

3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_TS_04_002_YSMP

docs.title: TS 00.001 – Overall Description
docs.text: 3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR_01_001.pdf

docs.title: TS 00.004 – Architecture
docs.text: 3GPP TS 23.501 – YesDR-00-004-architecture.pdf

docs.title: Validation and Debugging
docs.text: YesDR protocols can be validated using standard packet capture tools together with the provided Wireshark dissectors.

docs.title: Validation Workflow
docs.text:
1. Capture traffic between YUE, YBS, and YCore
  2. Save packets as PCAP
  3. Load PCAP in Wireshark
  4. Enable YACP / YSMP dissectors
  5. Verify message sequences against YesDR TS documents

docs.title: Supported Protocols
docs.text:
• YACP (Access Control)
  • YSMP (Spectrum Management)
